project_overview.project_name: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Hydropower and Renewable Energy Development (KHRE)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To increase renewable energy generation and strengthen the capacity of associated institutions in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
project_overview.beneficiaries: All electricity consumers in Pakistan; local communities in project areas; PEDO and GoKP; developers and financiers; estimated 1,100 GWh annual generation from Component A projects

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - KP province has experienced security challenges; project located in Swat Valley which has history of conflict but is now relatively stable; security considerations noted in risk assessment

key_components.component_name: Component A: Development of Hydropower and Renewable Energy Investments
key_components.description: Construction of hydropower and renewable energy projects including: A.1 Gabral-Kalam Hydropower Plant (88 MW run-of-river, 339 GWh annually); A.2 Cascade development of river basins including Madyan HPP (157 MW); A.3 Solar PV installation on hydropower assets (10-15 MW pilot)

key_components.component_name: Component B: Institutional Strengthening and Energy Sector Development
key_components.description: Planning and institutional strengthening including preparation of integrated plans, feasibility studies, design studies, and institutional capacity building for PEDO and E&P Department; support for portfolio development of future RE projects

key_components.component_name: Component C: Environment and Social Management
key_components.description: Implementation of resettlement action plans, social management plans including gender action plans, environmental management plans, and local area development through tourism, social services and electricity supply improvements

key_components.component_name: Component D: Project Implementation Support and Technical Assistance
key_components.description: Technical assistance, equipment, software, consulting services, Project Implementation Consultants, Independent Panel of Experts, training programs, and strategic studies

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Pakhtunkhwa Energy Development Organization (PEDO) on behalf of Government of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (GoKP); Energy and Power Department, Government of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee (PSC) chaired by Additional Chief Secretary with representation from relevant provincial departments (Energy, Planning, Finance, Forest, C&W, Tourism, Agriculture), Board of Revenue, District Commissioners, Ministry of Energy Power Division, and CEO PEDO; Project Management Organization (PMO) headed by Chief Engineer level officer reporting to CEO PEDO
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: International Competitive Bidding (ICB) for major works contracts (civil and electro-mechanical) following pre-qualification; Quality and Cost-Based Selection (QCBS) for three large consultancies (Project Implementation Consultants, Planning Consultants, Management Support Consultants); World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers (July 2016, revised August 2018); Project Procurement Strategy for Development (PPSD) prepared
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: PMO responsible for quarterly and annual progress reports to CEO PEDO, Secretary E&P, GoKP, and World Bank within 30 days of quarter end; Independent Panel of Experts (IPOE) to oversee design and construction; Project Implementation Consultants (PIC) to establish Management Information Systems and monitor physical progress; Management Support Consultants (MSC) to support M&E; Bi-annual Interim Unaudited Financial Reports (IUFRs) within 45 days of semester close; Annual audited financial statements by Auditor General of Pakistan within 6 months of fiscal year close

identified_risks.risk_description: COVID-19 pandemic impacts on economic stabilization, structural reforms, and commercial financing availability; circular debt in power sector estimated at PKR 2,154 billion (5.3% of GDP) with monthly increase of PKR 100 billion
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Commercial financing to be raised through consortium of banks in tranches over 2-3 years; World Bank financing structured to reduce risks for commercial financiers; interest in asset-backed Sukuks demonstrated from Dasu Hydropower Project experience; Government of Pakistan committed to structural reforms supported by WBG, IMF, and ADB

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited experience of PEDO in dealing with complex hydropower projects, procurement, financing, environment and social aspects; only 7 completed projects (157 MW total) since 1986; limited capacity for managing growing portfolio
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Component B provides institutional strengthening and capacity building; Management Support Consultants to support PEDO in project and contract management; Project Implementation Consultants for supervision; Independent Panel of Experts for technical oversight; Bank team regular site visits and monitoring; training programs for PMO, PEDO, and E&P Department staff

identified_risks.risk_description: Substantial fiduciary risk due to procurement of large value contracts (GKH civil works US$136 million, E&M US$46 million); complex contract management requirements
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Comprehensive Project Procurement Strategy for Development prepared; multiple layers of project supervision; Project Implementation Consultants supervising construction and contract management; Independent Panel of Experts with procurement and contract management expertise; robust oversight through PMO with dedicated procurement and contract management staff; World Bank procurement specialists providing training and support

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential environmental impacts including river ecology changes, fish habitat alteration, forest loss, construction-related dust/noise/waste; social impacts including land acquisition, resettlement of 89 households (8 physically displaced), loss of trees and crops; occupational health and safety risks from instream construction, tunnel work, and work at heights
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Detailed Environmental and Social Impact Assessment (ESIA) and Resettlement Action Plan (RAP) prepared for GKH; Environmental and Social Management Framework (ESMF) and Resettlement Policy Framework (RPF) for other projects; environmental flows maintained (2-12 cumecs depending on season); fish pass provided in weir; biodiversity studies and monitoring plans; Livelihood Restoration and Improvement Plan with US$1 million allocation; Social Development Plan with US$5 million for community benefits; Code of Conduct for contractors; OHS experts in PMO and supervision consultants; third-party monitoring of labor and working conditions

identified_risks.risk_description: Substantial risk from affected communities perceiving lack of benefit sharing and inadequate community engagement; land acquisition challenges due to lack of land records in area; potential for grievances and disputes
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Resettlement Action Plan includes Social Development Plan for benefit sharing; negotiated approach for land acquisition based on affected household demands; three-tier Grievance Redress Mechanism established; extensive stakeholder consultations (46 Focus Group Discussions with 249 men and 36 women, 5 information disclosure workshops, 7 consultative meetings); Stakeholder Engagement Framework and Plan; regular community engagement through Jirgas and local stakeholders; PEDO and PMO in regular contact with local communities; Gender Action Plan to promote women's voice and agency

identified_risks.risk_description: Political and governance risks related to provincial-level project implementation; coordination challenges between multiple government departments
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project Steering Committee with high-level representation to improve coordination; Additional Chief Secretary as PSC chair; formal notification of high-level committee for land rate negotiations; clear delegation of powers to Head PMO documented in Standard Operating Procedures

identified_risks.risk_description: Technical risks related to design adequacy, construction execution, and meeting project schedule; potential for construction delays and cost overruns
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Tender-level designs for GKH reviewed by Independent Panel of Experts; designs prepared according to international standards with hydrologic, hydraulic, seismic, and sedimentation analyses; mathematical models developed for spillway and outlets; Project Implementation Consultants for design review and construction supervision; IPOE to oversee design and construction quality; contingencies included in cost estimates; design optimization considering alternatives; experience from similar Matiltan HPP project

identified_risks.risk_description: Risk related to alignment with national energy policies and sector reforms; dependence on policy continuity for renewable energy development
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project aligned with Government's renewable energy policy and Least-Cost Generation Plan; supported by RISE Development Policy Loan (Prior Action 5 on reducing imported fuel dependency); planned PACE DPF series to support Renewable Energy Policy implementation; project contributes to national target of 66% renewable energy in mix by 2030

identified_risks.risk_description: Security risks related to law and order situation in KP province; potential impact on project implementation and staff safety
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Bidding documents to indicate provision of site security; specific security arrangements to be discussed at pre-bid meetings; project located in Swat Valley which is now relatively stable compared to past; OHS emphasis including staff and community safety programs

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: Yes - Three-tier Grievance Redress Mechanism established to provide time-bound, early, transparent and fair resolution of grievances; includes provisions for labor issues and Sexual Exploitation and Abuse/Sexual Harassment cases; World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS) also available; Inspection Panel available for complaints regarding World Bank non-compliance
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender Action Plan prepared as part of ESIA; target of 15% total female staff in PEDO by FY23 (baseline 2.6%); commitment to hire at least 30% women in PMO at Grade 17 and above (senior technical positions); PEDO to become WePOWER partner; targeted job outreach and advertisements; women staff in recruitment and interview panels; site visits and internship opportunities for female engineering students; job fairs and informational outreach; enabling policies including daycare facilities, safe transportation, and anti-harassment cell; Gender Action Plan monitoring through percentage of female staff and technical female staff indicators; schemes directly benefiting women targeted in results framework
vulnerable_groups: Affected households (89 households affected, 8 physically displaced)
Vulnerable and disadvantaged individuals in local communities
Downstream communities affected by river flow changes

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project located in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province which has experienced security challenges and conflict; Swat Valley has history of conflict but is now relatively stable; project design incorporates conflict sensitivity through: strong community engagement and benefit-sharing mechanisms to build local buy-in; Social Development Plan to address community needs and expectations; Grievance Redress Mechanism to address grievances early; employment opportunities for local population during construction and operation; improved infrastructure and services in underserved areas; security arrangements specified in bidding documents; project contributes to economic development and poverty reduction which can support stability
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: KP province faces fragility challenges including: history of conflict and security concerns; limited institutional capacity for project implementation; weak land records and governance systems; poverty and limited economic opportunities in project areas; limited access and underdeveloped infrastructure in remote project locations; conservative culture limiting women's participation; limited experience with large infrastructure projects at provincial level; macroeconomic vulnerabilities exacerbated by COVID-19 pandemic
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security risks identified as moderate; project located in Swat Valley which has experienced conflict but is now relatively stable; bidding documents to include site security provisions; specific security arrangements to be discussed at pre-bid meetings; law and order perception in province noted as risk factor; OHS programs include staff and community safety; project expected to have limited labor influx reducing security risks from migrant workers; conservative culture in region limits interaction between construction labor and communities
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: Yes - Involuntary Resettlement OP/BP 4.12 triggered; GKH will affect 171 households total, with 89 affected by permanent land use and remaining by temporary land use; 8 households will experience physical displacement due to loss of structures; 19 privately owned fruit trees affected; 636 wood/timber trees owned by Forest Department affected; Resettlement Action Plan prepared with negotiated approach for land acquisition; compensation at replacement cost for structures and market value for crops and trees; Livelihood Restoration and Improvement Plan with US$1 million allocation; Social Development Plan with US$5 million for community benefits; total RAP budget US$25.17 million; land acquisition and compensation costs borne by GoKP/PEDO; implementation to commence within 6 months of effectiveness
